title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Repository Trace: Handoff Runbook
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git handoff runbook increment in a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
Audit source identity, tree cleanliness, review range, checks, tag, artifact provenance, and remote references before release.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Handoff Runbook
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Handoff Runbook contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

content.text: A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Handoff Runbook condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

hints: Name the Handoff Runbook precondition or invariant that the theory case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation.

options.text: Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Handoff Runbook
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Handoff Runbook application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

content.text: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. A defensible solution applies Handoff Runbook, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 2
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation. This fails the Handoff Runbook contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: gitworkflows Manual (current as of 2026-07) — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

steps.title: Sequence Handoff Runbook evidence
steps.instruction: Order the Handoff Runbook evidence cycle for this theory task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Handoff Runbook assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Build Handoff Runbook evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-handoff-runbook-pe1fkt" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Handoff Runbook into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: git-handoff-runbook-pe1fkt
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.instruction:

A teammate acts on this belief: “Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation.” Which repair addresses the cause?

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Handoff Runbook, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Defend the Handoff Runbook model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Handoff Runbook: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v3.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
